about.title: About Dynai
about.body: Founded in 2004 and headquartered in the Nanjing Jiangbei New Materials Industrial Park, Dynai operates three manufacturing bases — Nanjing, Binhai and Maoming — specialising in the research, production and sale of ethylene-oxide and propylene-oxide derivatives. Years of innovation have made us the domestic leader in glycol ether and propylene glycol ether capacity with the most complete product portfolio in the industry.

page.heroSubtitle: Two decades of fine-chemistry leadership — engineered for a greener, higher-performing tomorrow.

profile.title: Two decades of applied chemistry, engineered for the world.
profile.lead: Founded in 2004 in the Nanjing Jiangbei New Materials Industrial Park, Dynai (Dena) has grown into a leading fine-chemical enterprise in China — the domestic leader in glycol ether and propylene glycol ether capacity with the industry's most complete product portfolio.
profile.body: We specialise in the research, production and sale of ethylene-oxide and propylene-oxide derivatives. Through continuous innovation, our product structure has steadily optimised and our profitability has grown year after year. We now serve green coatings, electronic-grade chemistry, semiconductor manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, cosmetics and construction markets across Asia, Europe and the Americas — guided by our mission to raise the quality of life through better chemistry.

highlights.title: Leading industry capacity
highlights.caption: Domestic #1 in glycol ether & propylene glycol ether output

items.name: Maoming Base
items.description: Southern China production hub commissioned in 2019, serving coastal and international customers.

culture.title: Cultivating virtue and learning, embracing all rivers.
culture.body: The spirit of Dana is shaped by our founder, Qin Yisheng (1947–2022) — a three-decade veteran of China's chemical industry. Recognised as a Jiangsu Provincial Model Worker (2001) and named China Township Entrepreneur (2004 & 2005), he held 19 patents and built a company ethos rooted in rigour, generosity and long-term thinking.

founder.name: Qin Yisheng
founder.quote: "The river accepts every stream. A company grows by lifting everyone with it."

items.title: Better chemistry for a better life.

rd.title: Research & Innovation
rd.body: R&D is one of Dynai's core competencies. We continue to expand research investment, collaborate with leading universities and research institutes, and attract outstanding talent. Today we operate a Jiangsu Provincial Engineering Technology R&D Centre, a postdoctoral research station and a joint university–industry centre, holding multiple proprietary key technologies.

items.title: Double-capped polyether platform advances to pilot scale
items.excerpt: Our proprietary double end-capped polyether technology completed its pilot-scale verification this quarter, delivering a measured step-change in surfactant performance and hydrolytic stability versus the incumbent benchmark.

items.title: Ultra-pure PGMEA lifts throughput at a tier-1 semiconductor customer

items.title: Low-odour coalescent wins national green-product award

items.title: Alert odour detection prevents a plant incident
items.subtitle: Operator Sun Teng commended after averting a potential release.
items.excerpt: On the morning of 8 April 2026, operators at Dynai's Nanjing Jiangbei base detected an unusual odour at the reactor module, reported it promptly and isolated the upstream line — averting a potential release.
items.author: Dana Editorial
body: At 06:42 on Tuesday, 8 April 2026, A-shift operator Sun Teng noted a faint, unfamiliar odour during a routine walkthrough of Reactor Bay 2 at our Nanjing Jiangbei base. Within sixty seconds she had activated the local gas-detection grid, paged the shift lead and de-energised the upstream feed pump.

A subsequent investigation traced the source to a pinhole leak on an auxiliary vapour-return line — a condition that would almost certainly have escalated without early intervention. No product was lost and no downstream unit was affected.

At the monthly EHS forum, Vice-President of Operations Wang Lei formally commended Sun Teng for her attentiveness and presence of mind. A small cash bonus was awarded alongside a site-wide memo on odour-trigger protocols.
The incident has since been folded into our quarterly safety refresher — a reminder that the best process safety systems are the ones built by the people closest to the equipment.

items.title: Spring winds and rising force — honouring the women of Dana
items.subtitle: A photo essay from the 116th International Women's Day celebrations.
items.excerpt: March winds and spring blossoms — to mark the 116th International Women's Day, Dana ran a week of workshops, mentoring sessions and a cross-base photography exhibition honouring the women who keep our chemistry running.
items.author: Dana Editorial
body: Between 5 and 8 March 2026, Dana's three production bases came alive with colour. Florists were invited on-site, laboratory teams presented five-minute talks on the women scientists who shaped modern chemistry, and a mentoring lunch was held for rising talent in both R&D and operations.

A cross-base photography exhibition — titled 'At Work' — travelled between Nanjing, Binhai and Maoming. It featured forty portraits: reactor operators, lab chemists, logistics coordinators and the plant nurse — each captioned by the subject themselves.

The week closed with a panel discussion chaired by VP of People Operations Zhang Yuan, on flexible scheduling, parental leave and pathways into technical leadership — conversations our editorial team will continue to surface throughout 2026.

items.title: Working together for a shared future — 2026 annual conference
items.subtitle: Four hundred colleagues gather to review 2025 and chart the year ahead.
items.excerpt: As 2025 drew to a close, approximately four hundred Dana colleagues gathered in Nanjing for the 2026 annual working conference — reviewing operational highlights and unveiling the roadmap for the year ahead.
items.author: Dana Editorial
body: On 5 February 2026, roughly four hundred colleagues from Nanjing, Binhai and Maoming gathered at the Jiangbei International Conference Centre for Dana's annual working conference — themed 'Working Together for a Shared Future'.

Chairman Qin Yonghua opened with a candid review of 2025: revenue up 14%, a Class-1000 cleanroom commissioned at the Maoming base, and our first semi-public ESG disclosure. He closed with a clear priority for 2026 — deepen product quality and widen our international partnerships.

Department leads followed with concrete commitments for the year, from a 30% reduction in logistics emissions to three new joint-lab programmes with regional universities. The conference ended with a long-service ceremony recognising thirty-two colleagues with ten, twenty or thirty years of service.

items.title: 2025 mid-level cadre performance review completed
items.subtitle: One hundred and twelve managers reviewed across three bases.
items.excerpt: Dana completed its annual mid-level cadre performance review in January 2026 — a structured, peer-assisted process covering one hundred and twelve managers across all three production bases.
items.author: Dana Editorial
body: Between 6 and 20 January 2026, Dana's People Operations and executive teams completed the annual mid-level cadre performance review. One hundred and twelve managers — across production, R&D, sales and support functions — were assessed on a balanced scorecard of delivery, leadership, safety culture and cross-base collaboration.

The review format was refreshed for 2025: each manager submitted a self-assessment, gathered feedback from three direct reports and two peers, and presented a fifteen-minute session to a cross-functional panel. The format took longer but produced materially richer development plans.
Outcomes were communicated in the first week of February, alongside individual growth pathways — a mix of rotational assignments, external coaching and targeted training.

items.title: Dana ranks among China's top-100 petrochemical private enterprises
items.subtitle: Industry ranking recognises two decades of focused, responsible growth.
items.excerpt: Dana Group has been listed among China's Top-100 Private Petrochemical Enterprises for 2025 — an external industry ranking that recognises steady, responsible growth across fine chemicals and electronic materials.
items.author: Dana Editorial
body: On 14 January 2026, the China Petroleum and Chemical Industry Federation released its annual ranking of the country's Top-100 Private Petrochemical Enterprises. Dana Group was included for the eighth consecutive year, moving up two places on the strength of 2025 revenue and sustainability disclosures.

Chairman Qin Yonghua accepted the recognition in Beijing, crediting 'the long-horizon discipline of our three production bases and the scientists who keep pushing our glycol-ether and electronic-grade portfolios forward.'

The ranking evaluates revenue, EBITDA, safety record, R&D investment as a share of revenue, and ESG disclosure quality. Dana was specifically highlighted for its Class-1000 cleanroom expansion and wet-electronic-chemicals growth.

items.title: Winter solstice dumplings — a warm gathering at Dana
items.subtitle: Canteen teams host a solstice dinner across all three bases.
items.excerpt: On the winter solstice, Dana's three bases hosted a joint dumpling dinner — a warm tradition that draws in operators, scientists and their families alike.
items.author: Dana Editorial
body: On Friday, 19 December 2025, all three Dana bases opened their canteens for an evening dumpling-making dinner. The solstice gathering has been a tradition at Dana since 2008 — it started in Nanjing and slowly travelled to Binhai and Maoming as the group grew.

This year's menu featured four regional fillings: three-fresh (Nanjing), shrimp-and-chive (Binhai), sour-cabbage pork (Shenyang dialect pairing for the Maoming team) and a vegetarian mushroom-walnut option prepared by the R&D kitchen's in-house vegetarian circle.
Proceeds from a small raffle alongside the dinner were donated to the Jiangbei Community Relief Fund — a cause the company has supported since 2013.

sustainability.title: Sustainability
sustainability.body: Dynai rigorously implements all relevant regulations, pursues safety-goal management, runs continuous safety training, and carries out risk identification, grading, and hazard investigation. The company is ISO 9001, ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 certified and has held the Jiangsu Province Level-2 Safety Production Standardisation certificate since 2009; we are currently pursuing Level-1 certification.

safety.title: Safety in production.
safety.lead: From traditional fine chemistry to a new-materials chemical enterprise — every step measured, every year independently audited.
body: Dana rigorously implements all relevant national, provincial and local safety and environmental regulations. We pursue safety-goal management, run continuous safety training, and carry out structured risk identification, hazard grading and incident investigation across all three production bases.

We are ISO 9001, ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 certified. We have held the Jiangsu Province Level-2 Safety Production Standardisation certificate since 2009 and are currently pursuing Level-1 certification. In 2017 we adopted the OSHA-style PSM (Process Safety Management) framework — every reactor unit since then has been designed, commissioned and reviewed under PSM principles.

Over the last decade we have transitioned from a traditional fine-chemical enterprise to a new-materials chemical enterprise — the same physical sites, but a deeper commitment to ultra-pure electronic chemicals, water-based resins and other lower-impact product lines.

items.label: VOC emissions intensity
items.sublabel: Per tonne of product, vs. 2020 baseline

items.body: Annual surveillance, three-yearly recertification audits — covering all manufacturing, R&D and customer-facing processes.
Documented EMS covering air, water, waste and materials — applied at every production base.
Replaces our prior OHSAS 18001 certification — same continuous-improvement discipline, broader risk lens.
Provincial standard certified annually since 2009 — currently in active pursuit of Level-1 status.
OSHA-style PSM framework adopted across every reactor unit — design, commissioning and operating reviews.
Voluntary chemical-industry framework integrating EHS performance with stakeholder transparency.

community.title: Beyond chemistry — investing in people and place.
community.lead: Over the past two decades, Dana has donated nearly 10 million yuan in support of education, poverty alleviation, and bridge and road construction across Jiangsu, Yancheng and Maoming.
community.projectsLead: A small selection of the work, with the impact numbers we measure ourselves on.

projects.title: Liuhe District scholarship programme
projects.body: Annual scholarship for top-performing high-school students from rural townships in Liuhe District. The programme covers tuition, books and travel, and assigns each scholar a Dana mentor for the duration of their university studies.

projects.title: Jiangbei community relief fund
projects.body: A reserve fund for hardship cases in our Jiangbei community — emergency medical care, post-disaster repairs and short-term housing support. Topped up annually from a combination of corporate donations and an opt-in employee giving programme.

projects.title: Maoming bridge & road improvement
projects.body: Five-year programme co-financed with the Maoming municipal government to upgrade the connecting road and three small bridges between our base and three nearby villages.

projects.title: Binhai coastal restoration
projects.body: Joint project with the Binhai Coastal Authority to plant native salt-tolerant grasses on a 12-hectare strip of degraded coastal land adjacent to our Binhai base.

community.ngoLead: Public-good organisations we work with year-round.

cta.title: Want to dig deeper?
cta.body: Our EHS and ESG teams publish quarterly disclosures and respond to all good-faith inquiries within five business days.

join.title: Build a career that matters.
join.body: Guided by the principle of attract-develop-inspire, we align human resources with the company's wider resources to unlock the full potential of our people — creating shared value for employees, the company, our customers and society.

page.heroTitle: Build a career that matters — together.
page.heroSubtitle: Across three production bases and a growing R&D footprint, we invest in the people who invest in us. This is our talent mechanism — from philosophy to benefits.

philosophy.title: Attract · Develop · Inspire.
philosophy.lead: The three verbs that shape how we hire, grow and reward the people who make Dana.

items.title: Attracting talent worth the long game.
items.body: We look for the curious and the conscientious — chemists who ask 'why', engineers who sweat the details, operators who speak up. We compete on work worth doing, teams worth joining, and leaders worth following — not on one-off signing bonuses.

items.title: Developing talent the patient way.
items.body: Apprenticeship programmes, internal rotations, and external certifications give every colleague a path. We pair early-career engineers with two mentors — one technical, one leadership — and review growth every six months, not once a year.

items.title: Inspiring talent with trust and ownership.
items.body: Performance is rewarded with autonomy, not just pay. Teams set their own quarterly goals, own their safety targets, and present their wins at the monthly all-hands. We hire adults and treat them that way.

development.title: Paths that open up as you grow.
development.lead: Three commitments we make to every colleague who joins Dana.

items.title: Diversified career paths
items.body: Dual tracks across technical and managerial progression — a senior scientist earns as much as a department head. Choose the path that fits you, switch when your interests do.

items.title: Internal competition first
items.body: Open roles are posted internally for two weeks before external hiring. In 2025, more than 40% of our mid-level promotions were filled by internal candidates.

items.title: Comprehensive training system
items.body: Apprenticeship, in-house academy, external lecturers, online and offline blended learning — with a per-person training budget reviewed every year and no cap on approved external courses.

benefits.title: Benefits after becoming a full-time employee.
benefits.lead: A complete package that takes care of the basics — and then some.

items.title: Five insurances + housing fund
items.body: Full statutory social insurance and housing fund contributions, managed locally at every base.

items.title: Supplemental medical insurance
items.body: Employer-paid top-up cover for inpatient and major-illness scenarios, extendable to family.

items.title: Dormitory
items.body: On-site dormitory option for colleagues relocating or commuting long distance.

items.title: Shuttle bus
items.body: Shuttle routes from the nearest metro or city hubs to every production base.

items.title: Canteen
items.body: Two subsidised hot meals a day with rotating regional menus and vegetarian options.

items.title: Holiday benefits
items.body: Lunar New Year, Mid-Autumn and Dragon Boat gifts; family days twice a year.

items.title: Birthday benefits
items.body: A personalised birthday card from your team lead and a catered birthday cake on the day.

items.title: Hospital visits
items.body: Paid leave and a welfare fund contribution when colleagues are hospitalised.

items.title: Wedding & funeral support
items.body: Cash and paid leave for major life events — weddings, funerals and the birth of a child.

testimonials.title: People who make it worth showing up.
testimonials.lead: Three of our colleagues on what keeps them at Dana.

items.quote: My first rotation was three months in the R&D lab. My second was a month on the night shift. That's unusual — and it made me a better process engineer.
items.name: Chen Wei

items.quote: I've worked at three Chinese chemical companies. Dana is the first one that listened when I pushed back on a safety design.
items.name: Liu Xi

items.quote: When I told my team I wanted to move from QC to the analytical lab, my manager spent a month building the transition plan with me. I didn't even have to ask twice.
items.name: Wang Tao

dayInLife.title: Eight stops with a Dana process engineer.
dayInLife.lead: Chen Wei walks us through a Tuesday at the Nanjing Jiangbei base.

items.body: Jumped on the shuttle from the Luhe metro — 25 minutes reading time, one seat away from my reactor bay.
Fifteen-minute handover with the night shift. Two stable batches, one minor pump alarm to watch.
Steamed buns and soy milk from the canteen. I owe a colleague a bowl of noodles for last week.
Bay-by-bay walk-through with the operations lead. Spotted a steam-trap needing replacement this afternoon.
Quick lunch — fish, vegetables and rice — with the analytical team. A quarterly budget conversation slides into a casual mentoring session.
Monthly one-on-one with my technical mentor. We revise my Q2 development plan together.
Unannounced gas-leak drill at bay 3. Nine minutes from alarm to all-clear — a personal best for the shift.
Shuttle back to the metro. Tomorrow: writing up the steam-trap investigation and a short training session with two new hires.

socialItems.title: Sales Manager
responsibilities: Own the full sales cycle for assigned accounts and territories — pipeline, contract, payment.
Build and maintain relationships with both new and key existing customers.
Coordinate with operations, R&D and logistics to deliver on commitments.
Maintain accurate forecasts and CRM hygiene; report monthly performance to the regional lead.
Represent Dana at industry events and customer-site visits across China and select export markets.
Feed market intelligence back to product management and pricing.
requirements: 3+ years of B2B sales experience in fine chemicals, coatings, electronic chemicals or adjacent industries.
Bachelor's degree (Marketing, Applied Chemistry or Polymer Materials preferred); willingness to travel up to 40%.

socialItems.title: DCS Operator
responsibilities: Operate the distributed control system across assigned production units, maintaining safe and stable operation within set parameters.
Monitor and adjust real-time process variables; respond to alarms following SOPs.
Carry out shift handover discipline — written log, verbal handover, walk-through.
Participate in scheduled drills, hazop reviews and continuous-improvement projects.
requirements: Junior college diploma or above in chemical engineering or process control.
Operator certificate; willingness to work rotating shifts at the Binhai base.
1+ year of DCS experience preferred — graduate hires considered with strong simulator scores.

socialItems.title: Organic Synthesis Research Group Leader
responsibilities: Lead an organic-synthesis research team focused on EO/PO derivatives and selective functionalisation chemistry.
Set the technical roadmap, project portfolio and scale-up readiness criteria with R&D leadership.
Mentor scientists and graduate students; collaborate with university partners on joint-lab projects.
Translate lab-scale wins into pilot and commercial production with the process-engineering team.
requirements: PhD in organic, medicinal or applied chemistry; outstanding MS candidates with a strong publication record will be considered.
Demonstrated track record leading a small research team (3+ scientists).
Hands-on synthesis expertise — particularly in alkoxylation, esterification or selective functional-group transformations.
Comfortable in a bilingual (Mandarin / English) research environment.
